text:
A long time ago, not far from a sleepy little town called Willow Creek, there was a young boy named Jamie. Jamie had a passion only a few could understand—the art of collecting bird feathers.

The forest around Willow Creek was alive with songbirds singing their morning songs, and Jamie would spend hours hunting the softest feathers for his collection. His friends didn't get it—their hobbies were louder, more obvious. Jamie liked tranquility. He didn't want loud music, flashy dresses, or parties. He wanted solitude with his collection.

One afternoon, he discovered something amazing up in the dense thicket: a bird whose feathers were shimmering like diamonds under the sun. It was a plumage he had never seen before. He followed its tracks home, hoping to catch just one feather.

When he finally found the bird, it was perched on a high branch, watching him approach. Jamie didn't know what he was getting himself into. The bird flew away. But as he chased
